title: centaurea × serresii
intro: Centaurea × serresii, also known as Serres' knapweed, is a hybrid plant of the Centaurea genus, characterized by its clusters of small, blue to purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. These medicinal properties have been associated with various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Centaurea × serresii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
